---
description: "Goals and site standards for the Conan Exiles Enhanced web project."
applyTo: "projects/conan-exiles-enhanced/**"
---

# Conan Exiles Enhanced Goals

## Product Direction

- Build Conan Exiles Enhanced as a fully functional addition to the main monorepo site.
- Keep the site structure, interaction style, and overall visual language aligned with the other projects in this repository.
- Favor a strong information architecture over novelty. The site should feel like a purpose-built reference tool first.

## Site Standards

- Use the established project shell pattern: left side-panel navigation, top header bar, and responsive mobile fallback.
- Keep navigation stable across major sections so users can move between Home, Lore, Items, Checklists, and future map tools without relearning the layout.
- Optimize for dense but readable information display. This project should feel closer to a modern game database than a marketing site.
- Prefer progressive delivery. Ship the reference and search surfaces first, then add richer exploration features like map tooling.
- Treat local storage as the persistence layer for user-side preferences and unlock state until a stronger requirement appears.
- Keep settings centralized. User-configurable values like lore unlocks, crafting multipliers, fuzzy search, and favorites should be surfaced in a consistent settings area instead of scattered controls.
- Design data contracts so the website can consume static outputs generated by the Conan data workspace without runtime server dependencies.
- Use clear labels for game-world distinctions such as Exiled Lands versus Isle of Siptah. Do not hide those distinctions in ambiguous metadata.
- Favor accessible filtering, search, and comparison patterns. Keyboard access, readable contrast, and predictable control behavior matter more than flashy interactions.

## Delivery Priorities

- Prioritize the content and interaction model that unlocks the core companion experience.
- Build in this order unless the user redirects scope:
  1. Home page and navigation shell
  2. Lore unlocker and searchable lore browser
  3. Checklist surfaces for Journey and Sorcery
  4. Item browser and settings model
  5. Higher-complexity map and discovery tools

## Checklists

- Add a top-level Checklists section ahead of Map Tools in the site information architecture.
- Stage Journey and Sorcery first.
- Treat Journey as a checklist surface driven by extracted journey steps and, when available, unlock requirements.
- Treat Sorcery as a checklist surface that begins with Journey-related onboarding but progresses through Thaumaturgy Bench systems afterward.
- Sorcery costs should align with the same crafting cost multiplier model already used for the item browser and settings.
- Do not block checklist page staging on having the full dataset. Placeholder surfaces are acceptable while the extraction pipeline catches up.

## Look And Feel

- Keep the look and feel similar to the other projects in the repo.
- Include side-panel navigation and a header bar.
- Use a layout that stays readable with long-form item and lore content.
- Prefer a grounded, practical presentation over ornamental fantasy styling.

## Home Page

- Make the landing page easy to read and easy to navigate.
- Surface the major sections clearly from the home page.
- Use the sidebar pattern established by the other projects.
- Treat the home page as an orientation layer that explains what the companion can do and how to reach the main tools quickly.

## Item Browser

- Build the item browser as a wiki-style reference surface with stronger search and filtering than a typical wiki.
- Include both craftable and non-craftable items.
- The item detail experience should support, when data exists:
  - Crafting recipe
  - Description
  - Obtain methods
  - Map-specific obtain methods when applicable
  - Exiled Lands versus Isle of Siptah exclusivity
  - Thrall bonuses from T1 through T4
  - T4 specialty variations when they materially change stats
  - Enemy drops and, when practical, where those enemies are found
- Use a small but clear visual treatment for map exclusivity, such as a badge, watermark, or compact marker that does not overwhelm the core item data.
- Include a crafting cost multiplier setting.
- The multiplier should behave as literal component cost multiplied by the chosen value, with flooring behavior when the game does the same.
- Store the crafting multiplier in local storage.
- Include an option for fuzzy search.
- When fuzzy search is enabled, allow search to include description text in addition to item names and other primary metadata.

## Lore Unlocker And Browser

- Support the following lore types:
  - Notes and journals
  - NPC dialogue
  - Lorestones or steles
- Build the lore section as a searchable browser with user-controlled unlock state.
- Group related lore together in meaningful series or ownership buckets.
- Journal series should remain grouped, such as numbered journal sets.
  - Unlocks are on a per-entry basis, but grouping should make it clear when entries belong to a series.
- NPC dialogue should be grouped under the speaking NPC.
- Link related lore, NPCs, and items when those relationships are useful and supported by the data.
- Searching should search inside the lore text, not only titles.
- Search results should expose surrounding context text when practical.
- Only unlocked lore should be searchable and readable in the main lore experience.
- Use a modal or similar focused UI to manage unlock state from a searchable list.
- The manage-unlocks surface should support the same core search and filtering workflow as the main lore browser rather than behaving like a separate reduced-capability list.
- The default behavior is that lore starts locked until unlocked.
- A checkbox or equivalent toggle should unlock or enable a specific lore entry.
- Provide an unlock-all option in settings.
- Keep unlock-all separate from the user’s hand-selected unlocked lore state so one does not overwrite the other.
- Store unlocked lore state in local storage.
- Include a settings action to reset lore unlocks.
- Allow users to favorite certain notes, note series, or dialogue groups.

## Wish Goals

- A Google Maps-style browser similar in spirit to mapgenie.io.
- Allow unlocking of notes and NPCs through the map experience.
- Show resource locations.
- Use the example HTML in `documentation/mapgenie/` as research input rather than as a direct implementation constraint.
- Support different maps for Exiled Lands and Isle of Siptah.

## Standards For Implementation

- Keep major user-facing state explicit and recoverable: settings, unlocked lore, favorites, selected map, and search mode should be easy to reason about.
- Prefer modular section ownership. Home, Lore, Items, Checklists, and future Map features should each have a clear state owner and data contract.
- Avoid burying key game metadata in presentation-only code. Source-of-truth distinctions like map exclusivity, thrall tiering, and obtain methods should come from structured data.
- Build search with room to grow. The first implementation should be simple, but the data shape should not block richer indexing later.
- Favor stable URLs and sharable section entry points where practical.
- Keep the project compatible with the monorepo’s static-export deployment model.
- Defer formatting polish and low-priority presentation bug fixing while the lore extraction and staging pipeline is still settling. Prefer locking data shape and interaction requirements first, then revisit formatting cleanup afterward.
